title: 10 possible side effects of Turnera diffusa (Damiana) if used improperly
intro: Turnera diffusa, also known as damiana, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. When used improperly or in excess, Turnera diffusa can have several potential negative health effects. The most common side effects include stomach discomfort, nausea, and vomiting. In some cases, it may also cause constipation or diarrhea. Prolonged use or high doses of Turnera diffusa can lead to more serious complications, such as liver damage or interference with certain medications. Additionally, it may have hormonal effects and could potentially impact fertility in both men and women, although more research is needed in this area. Therefore, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using Turnera diffusa or any other supplement to ensure safe and appropriate use.
